instruction: Score this FLS Task 5 intracorporeal suture attempt. Analyze the frames and produce a structured scoring result.
input:
Frame 1 (needle_load): At 5 seconds, instrument in field from upper right near drain. Blue suture visible extending downward. Needle loading underway — extremely fast entry.
Frame 4 (suture_placement): At 22 seconds, both instruments crossing at drain. Needle driving in progress. Active, coordinated instrument work.
Frame 7 (first_throw): At 38 seconds, suture placed through drain. Instruments positioned for first throw wrapping. Transitioning from placement to throws.
Frame 9 (first_throw): At 49 seconds, first throw tightening. Both instruments pulling suture in opposite directions.
Frame 11 (second_throw): At 60 seconds, second throw wrapping. Both instruments crossing near drain with suture visible.
Frame 14 (third_throw): At 77 seconds, third throw completing or transitioning to cutting. Knot X pattern visible on drain. Suture loop above drain.
Frame 17 (suture_cut): At 93 seconds, cutting phase. Left instrument across drain holding suture. Right instrument (scissors) at lower right. Suture tails visible.
Frame 21 (suture_cut): At 106 seconds, still actively cutting. Both instruments near knot area. Suture tails being trimmed.
Frame 23 (completion): At 109 seconds (final frame), instruments being withdrawn. Knot visible on drain with suture tails. Drain slit partially closed. Drain not avulsed, remains on block. Video ends at 109.4 seconds — task appears to complete at approximately 107 seconds.

Bilateral tightening visible. Confidence low-moderate due to monitor quality."}, {"throw_number": 2, "is_surgeon_knot": null, "is_single_throw": true, "hand_used": "unclear", "hand_switched": true, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "Second throw at 60s \u2014 fast 14-second throw. Confidence low."}, {"throw_number": 3, "is_surgeon_knot": null, "is_single_throw": true, "hand_used": "unclear", "hand_switched": true, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "Third throw completed in 16 seconds \u2014 fastest in the series. Confidence low."}], "suture_placement": {"deviation_from_mark1_mm": 2.0, "deviation_from_mark2_mm": 2.0, "total_deviation_penalty": 4.0, "confidence": "very_low"}, "drain_assessment": {"gap_visible": true, "drain_avulsed": false, "slit_closure_quality": "partial", "assessment_note": "Monitor recording limits gap assessment. Drain slit appears partially closed but a small gap may be present. The speed increase may come at slight quality cost compared to V13. Confidence very low."}, "estimated_penalties": 6.0, "estimated_fls_score": 487.0, "technique_summary": "V14 sets a NEW ALL-TIME BEST at approximately 107 seconds \u2014 the first time under 110 seconds. The total throw sequence at 48 seconds matches V12's record. Drain closure appears partial (slight quality trade-off for speed), but the time gains far outweigh the estimated penalty increase. Sub-100 seconds is now within reach."}

instruction: Score this FLS Task 5 intracorporeal suture attempt. Analyze the frames and produce a structured scoring result.
input:
Frame 1 (idle): Drain visible on pink foam block with white tape band. Penrose drain centered with yellow/green opening visible. No instruments in field. Two mark dots barely visible on drain — hard to see on monitor recording. Clean starting position.
Frame 2 (needle_load): First instrument entering from upper right. Curved needle with suture visible — dark suture thread extending from instrument tip down toward drain. Second instrument partially visible at upper left. TIMING STARTS approximately 6 seconds.
Frame 3 (needle_load): Right needle driver approaching drain with loaded needle. Dark suture line visible from tip to drain area. Left instrument entering from upper left. Positioning for needle pass.
Frame 4 (suture_placement): Active needle pass through drain. Curved needle clearly visible arcing from center through drain tissue. White suture trailing from needle. Left instrument (grasper) stabilizing drain from right side. Both instruments fully engaged.
Frame 5 (suture_placement): Needle driving continues. Both instruments converging on drain. Curved needle visible with white suture extending from both sides of drain — suture passing through first mark. Left instrument holding drain, right instrument driving needle.
Frame 6 (suture_placement): Needle has passed through drain. Both suture ends now visible extending from drain — one going upper left, one going lower right. Instruments positioned at drain level pulling suture through. Needle visible at instrument tip. Suture placement through both marks nearing completion.
Frame 7 (first_throw): Suture placement complete. Instruments repositioning above drain. Suture ends visible extending from drain to instruments. Needle visible at upper left held by left instrument. Right instrument holding other suture end. Preparing for first throw wrap.
Frame 8 (first_throw): First throw (surgeon's knot) wrapping in progress. Both instruments above drain. Suture being wrapped around one instrument for the double throw. Loop of suture visible between instrument tips. Dark suture line extending from wrap to drain.
Frame 9 (first_throw): First throw tightening. Both instruments at drain level pulling suture tight. Knot beginning to form at drain surface. Both instruments pulling in opposite directions. Suture crossing visible at drain center.
Frame 10 (second_throw): Second throw preparation. Both instruments above drain, repositioning for next wrap. Suture loop visible at right side of drain. Dark suture extending upward to instruments. First throw appears complete and seated.
Frame 11 (second_throw): Second throw wrapping/tightening. Both instruments above drain pulling suture. Multiple suture lines visible. Left instrument grasping suture from left side (consistent with hand switch from first throw). Knot building at drain.
Frame 12 (second_throw): Second throw tightening. Both instruments near drain level. Suture crossed above drain. Left instrument from left, right instrument from right. Knot visible at drain surface.
Frame 13 (third_throw): Third throw in progress. Left instrument from upper left. Right instrument from lower right. Suture wrapping visible with loop at right side. Knot mass building up at drain center.
Frame 14 (third_throw): Third throw continuing. Both instruments near knot. Large suture loops visible on and around drain surface. Multiple wraps evident. Instruments pulling and tightening.
Frame 15 (third_throw): Third throw tightening. Both instruments at drain pulling. Knot visible at drain center. Drain slightly pinched/compressed by knot tension. Suture ends visible.
Frame 16 (third_throw): Third throw completing. Both instruments at drain. Knot seated. Suture tails extending. Green highlight visible at knot area on monitor.
Frame 17 (suture_cut): Scissors clearly visible — serrated edge of laparoscopic scissors from upper left. Text/markings on instrument shaft. Right instrument at drain. Positioned near first suture tail.
Frame 18 (suture_cut): Active cutting. Scissors at lower left of drain. Knot visible at center with suture wraps. One tail being cut. Second instrument providing counter-traction.
Frame 19 (suture_cut): Both instruments widely separated. Knot visible at drain center. Suture tails splayed outward, still relatively long. Drain slit appears mostly CLOSED around knot — good approximation visible.
Frame 20 (suture_cut): Single instrument visible from upper left. Knot clearly visible with crossing pattern — compact. Tails present but shorter than at 150s. Progress being made on cuts.
Frame 21 (suture_cut): NOTABLE: Blue IRWIN-branded instrument now visible from lower right alongside gray laparoscopic instrument. Both at drain. Knot visible. White suture tails still present.
Frame 22 (completion): Final frame. Blue IRWIN and gray instruments at drain. Knot compact with short stubs. Drain slit mostly closed. Drain not avulsed, centered on block. Task completing as video ends at 177.4s.
output: {"completion_time_seconds": 170.0, "phase_timings": [{"phase": "idle", "start_seconds": 0.0, "end_seconds": 6.0, "duration_seconds": 6.0}, {"phase": "needle_load", "start_seconds": 6.0, "end_seconds": 14.0, "duration_seconds": 8.0}, {"phase": "suture_placement", "start_seconds": 14.0, "end_seconds": 36.0, "duration_seconds": 22.0}, {"phase": "first_throw", "start_seconds": 36.0, "end_seconds": 58.0, "duration_seconds": 22.0}, {"phase": "second_throw", "start_seconds": 58.0, "end_seconds": 78.0, "duration_seconds": 20.0}, {"phase": "third_throw", "start_seconds": 78.0, "end_seconds": 110.0, "duration_seconds": 32.0}, {"phase": "suture_cut", "start_seconds": 110.0, "end_seconds": 175.0, "duration_seconds": 65.0}], "knot_assessments": [{"throw_number": 1, "is_surgeon_knot": true, "is_single_throw": null, "hand_used": "right", "hand_switched": null, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "First throw 36-58s (22s). Right instrument leads wrap. Double wrap visible at 44s consistent with surgeon's knot. Tightened by 52-56s. Confidence: low \u2014 monitor recording, white suture."}, {"throw_number": 2, "is_surgeon_knot": null, "is_single_throw": true, "hand_used": "left", "hand_switched": true, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "Second throw 58-78s (20s). Left instrument leads (hand switch confirmed). Single wrap. Efficient throw. Confidence: low."}, {"throw_number": 3, "is_surgeon_knot": null, "is_single_throw": true, "hand_used": "right", "hand_switched": true, "appears_secure": true, "notes": "Third throw 78-110s (32s) \u2014 LONGEST single throw. Right instrument leads (hand switch confirmed). Extended duration with large loops at 92s. May indicate difficulty grasping or wrapping. Confidence: low."}], "suture_placement": {"deviation_from_mark1_mm": 2.0, "deviation_from_mark2_mm": 2.0, "total_deviation_penalty": 4.0, "confidence": "very_low"}, "drain_assessment": {"gap_visible": false, "drain_avulsed": false, "slit_closure_quality": "appears_closed", "assessment_note": "At 150s, drain slit appears mostly closed around knot \u2014 good approximation. Knot compact at 164-175s. No visible gap from monitor view. Confidence LOW. Suture tails moderate length \u2014 not as long as V20's catastrophic tails. Drain centered on block, not avulsed."}, "estimated_penalties": 6.0, "estimated_fls_score": 424.0, "technique_summary": "V21 at an estimated 170 seconds is a moderate recovery from the V18-V20 fatigue crash sequence. This is a MONITOR RECORDING at the skills lab. Suture placement at 22 seconds is EXCELLENT \u2014 matching the V14 series peak and dramatically better than V19 (43s) and V20 (40s), confirming placement regression was fatigue-related not skill loss. Throws at 74 seconds total are mid-range (peak: 48s), with the third throw at 32 seconds being the longest phase. The CUTTING PHASE at 65 seconds (37% of total time) matches V19's worst-ever and continues the series-wide structural bottleneck. A blue IRWIN-branded external instrument appears in the final frames \u2014 possible protocol deviation. The absence of suture debris on the tape suggests this is an early session attempt, not a late-session fatigue crash. Overall, V21 demonstrates solid fundamental technique retention with a persistent cutting bottleneck that requires dedicated isolated practice."}
